name: Pasta & Variations
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the flour in a mound on a large flat surface.
2. Make a well in the center and add water and salt.
3. Using a fork, gently incorporate the flour from the side of the well into the liquid mixture.
4. Continue until the dough becomes sticky and difficult to work with the fork. Knead by hand to form a rough-looking dough.
5. Let the dough rest for 10 minutes. Knead the dough until most of the flour is used and the dough is smooth and elastic, about 10 minutes.
6. Divide the dough into 3 or 4 balls and place them in a plastic bag; set aside to rest for 30 minutes before rolling by hand.
7. Roll out one ball at a time to the desired thickness and cut into the desired shape and width.
Variations:
- Beet Pasta: Drain 1 (8 oz) can of cooked sliced beets, puree in a blender with 1 tbsp vegetable oil. Make a well in 2-1/2 cups all-purpose or unbleached flour, spoon beet puree into the well, add 1 tsp salt, and continue as directed.
- Broccoli Pasta: Cook 10 oz frozen chopped broccoli; drain. Puree in a blender with 1 room temperature large egg. Make a well in 2-1/2 cups all-purpose or unbleached flour, spoon broccoli puree into the well, add 1 tsp salt, and continue as directed.
- Egg Noodle Pasta: Make a well in 2-1/4 cups all-purpose or unbleached flour, break 3 room temperature large eggs into the well, add 1 tsp salt. Beat with a fork about 10 strokes before working in flour, then continue as directed.
- Egg Pasta: Make a well in 3 cups all-purpose, unbleached semolina or whole wheat flour, break 4 room temperature large eggs into the well, add 1 tbsp vegetable oil, 1 tbsp water, and 1/2 tsp salt. Beat with a fork about 10 strokes before working in flour, then continue as directed.
- Lemon Pasta: Make a well in 2 cups all-purpose or unbleached flour, add 1/3 cup lemon juice, grated peel of 2 lemons, and 1/2 tsp salt to the well. Continue as directed.
- Onion Pasta: Make a well in 2-1/2 cups all-purpose or unbleached flour, break 4 room temperature large eggs into the well, add 2 tbsp vegetable oil and 1 package dry onion soup mix. Beat into the well about 10 strokes before working in flour, then continue as directed.
- Pineapple Pasta: Make a well in 2-1/4 cups all-purpose or unbleached flour, gently add thawed frozen pineapple juice concentrate and 1/4 cup sugar to the well. Beat with a fork about 10 strokes before working in flour, then continue as directed.
- Semolina Flour Pasta: Make a well in 2 cups semolina flour, add 1/2 cup + 3 tbsp water, 2 tbsp vegetable oil, and 1 tsp salt to the well. Continue as directed.
- Spicy Italian Pasta: Make a well in 2 cups all-purpose or unbleached flour, break 1 room temperature egg into the well, add 1/4 cup water, 2 tbsp vinegar, 1 tbsp vegetable oil, and 1 package dry Italian salad dressing mix. Beat with a fork about 10 strokes before working in flour, then continue as directed.
- Spinach Pasta: Cook 10 oz fresh or frozen spinach; drain. Puree in a blender with 1 room temperature large egg. Make a well in 3 cups all-purpose or unbleached flour, break another room temperature large egg into the well, add 1-1/2 tsp salt. Beat with a fork about 10 strokes, add spinach puree, and beat before working in flour.
- Whole Wheat Pasta: Make a well in 2 cups sifted whole-wheat flour, break 2 room temperature large eggs into the well, add 2-3 tbsp water, 1 tbsp vegetable oil, and 1 tsp salt. Beat with a fork about 10 strokes before working in flour, then continue as directed.
- Zucchini Pasta: Cook 10 oz cubed fresh zucchini in 1/2 cup boiling water until tender, drain. Puree in a blender with 1 room temperature large egg and 1 tbsp vegetable oil. Make a well in 4 cups all-purpose or unbleached flour, break another room temperature large egg into the well, add 2 tsp sugar and 1 tsp salt. Beat with a fork about 10 strokes, add zucchini puree, and beat before working in flour.
Yield: Makes about 6 servings
Source: Sophie Kay's Pasta Cookery
